title: dianthus cintranus subsp. atrosanguineus
intro: Dianthus cintranus subsp. atrosanguineus, also known as the 'Atrosanguineus' or 'Black-Flowered' Chrysanthemum or more commonly 'Red Spider Flower', is a plant species in the carnation family with deep red to almost black flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Dianthus cintranus subsp. atrosanguineus include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and combat infections. Dianthus cintranus subsp. atrosanguineus can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
